summary: Phase 18 backend tested via pytest (payout_schedule unification + commission_terms persistence + CL-XXXXX lookup + Mini-CRM public_code integration). 12 tests passed / 2 skipped (user-login for cross-role 403 probe). Frontend verified via deep code review: CommissionBreakdownModal + BusinessRegisterPage step 4 + UserDashboardPage badge + BusinessClientsTab chip + api.js wrapper all match spec. Legacy payout-triday/biweekly/monthly testids confirmed removed.

minor.issue: Could not login user test@example.com with common passwords (Test123!/TestPass123!/Password123!/password). Two tests (test_users_me_exposes_public_code, test_lookup_403_non_business) were SKIPPED as a result. The endpoint itself is fine — credential in problem statement may be stale. Seed user or expose password in /app/memory/test_credentials.md so cross-role auth can be proven.

code_review_findings: CommissionBreakdownModal.jsx: COMMISSION_TERMS_VERSION='v1-2026-02', BOOKVIA_FEE_MXN=8.20, stripeFee=deposit*0.085, clientPays=deposit+bookviaFee, businessReceives=deposit-stripeFee — matches spec exactly.
BusinessRegisterPage.jsx line 346: blocks nextStep when requires_deposit && !commission_terms_accepted with toast 'Debes leer y aceptar el desglose de comisiones para continuar' — correct.
No occurrences of payout-triday / payout-biweekly / payout-monthly anywhere in /app/frontend/src (grep confirmed) — legacy selector fully removed.
UserDashboardPage.jsx: user-public-code-badge present with label 'Tu ID' + clipboard copy + 'Código copiado' toast.
BusinessClientsTab.jsx: clients-search-input placeholder includes 'código CL-XXXXX' (line 113) and client-code-<public_code> chip on each ClientRow (line 242).

action_items: (Optional) Provide a working password for test@example.com (or create a dedicated TEST_user_no_history@bookvia.com) so the 403-on-user-token path can be asserted automatically.
No functional bugs — Phase 18 backend + frontend code look production-ready for the pre-launch.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/businesses.py::lookup_client_by_code correctly rejects BV- prefix, short codes, and invalid-alphabet chars via normalize_public_code + prefix guard — verified by tests.

routers/auth.py lines 535-537 force payout_schedule='monthly_cutoff_20' ONLY when requires_deposit=True — for non-deposit businesses it saves None. Migration covered existing docs per problem statement. This asymmetry is fine but worth noting: a business that later flips requires_deposit from false→true via /me PUT will have payout_schedule stay None unless the caller sends it (BusinessUpdate supports it). Consider backfilling payout_schedule='monthly_cutoff_20' server-side whenever requires_deposit is set to True.
routers/businesses.py::list_my_clients includes public_code only when the underlying user has one; walk-in clients return None — matches spec.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed; 2 skipped due to missing user creds)
success_rate.frontend: 100% code review — no live UI automation run for step 4 because the 5-step registration requires uploads; all testids and logic verified in source

test_credentials: testspa@test.com / Test123! worked for business flows. Could NOT login test@example.com with listed passwords.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 18 green. Backend lookup endpoint validated for 200/400 (BV- prefix, short, bad alphabet)/404/401 paths. Mini-CRM list + search by CL code validated. If next iteration adds more fields (e.g., commission_terms_accepted_at exposed on /businesses/me/private-info) extend the pytest file. For step-4 UI live automation, you'll need to either fake file uploads or add a dev-only query param that skips to step 4.
